---
output:
  beamer_presentation:
    theme: "CambridgeUS"
    colortheme: "dolphin"
    fonttheme: "structurebold"
    df_print: kable
fontsize: 14pt
classoption: "aspectratio=169"
header-includes:
- \usepackage{caption}
- \captionsetup[figure]{labelformat=empty}
- \captionsetup[table]{labelformat=empty}
- \setbeamertemplate{page number in head/foot}[]{}
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
## Render the pdf
##rmarkdown::render(input = "./07_1-Project2_Sec3.Rmd", output_file = "./07_1-Project2_Sec3.pdf")

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```


## Today's Agenda

\Large
Work on Section 3 of the Data Project 2 Report

\vspace{.5in}

\begin{center}
Justin Leinaweaver (Fall 2021)
\end{center}


## {.plain}
\center
\includegraphics[keepaspectratio=true,width=.95\paperwidth]{Images/00-Pizza_Politics2.png}


## Data Project 2: NMC
\large
\center
Prepare a report \textbf{introducing and analyzing} our second data project. 

\normalsize
### Sections (By Argument)
1. Why is this project important?
2. How confident should we be in the methodology?
3. **What does the world look like according to this data in 2016?**
4. How is the world changing across time according to this data?
5. Based on your analyses how can we best use this data project to understand the world?



## Brainstorming Section 3: Ideas to Test? {.plain}
\small

+ `cinc`: The Composite Index of National Capability (CINC) score

+ `irst`: Iron and steel production (thousands of tons)

+ `milex`: Military Expenditures (1816-1913 thousands GBP, since 1914 thousands USD)

+ `milper`: Military Personnel (thousands)

+ `tpop`: Total Population (thousands)

+ `upop`: Urban population (pop in cities > than 100,000; in thousands)

+ `pec`: Primary energy consumption (thousands of coal-ton equivalents)


## Brainstorming Section 3: Ideas to Test? {.plain}
\small

+ `cinc`: The Composite Index of National Capability (CINC) score
+ `irst`: Iron and steel production (thousands of tons)
+ `milex`: Military Expenditures (1816-1913 thousands GBP, since 1914 thousands USD)
+ `milper`: Military Personnel (thousands)
+ `tpop`: Total Population (thousands)
+ `upop`: Urban population (pop in cities > than 100,000; in thousands)
+ `pec`: Primary energy consumption (thousands of coal-ton equivalents)

### Task 1: 2016 Only

+ Visualize each with a histogram

+ Map CINC



## Brainstorming Section 3: Ideas to Test? {.plain}
\small

+ `cinc`: The Composite Index of National Capability (CINC) score
+ `irst`: Iron and steel production (thousands of tons)
+ `milex`: Military Expenditures (1816-1913 thousands GBP, since 1914 thousands USD)
+ `milper`: Military Personnel (thousands)
+ `tpop`: Total Population (thousands)
+ `upop`: Urban population (pop in cities > than 100,000; in thousands)
+ `pec`: Primary energy consumption (thousands of coal-ton equivalents)

### Task 2: 2016 Only
+ Re-build the CINC score manually

+ Build a new CINC score with a different weighting
